input.new_string:
\textbf{Abelian sheafification preserves the coequalizer.} The abelian
  sheafification functor \(a\) (the associated-sheaf functor for \(J\) on
  abelian-group presheaves) is a left adjoint, hence preserves coequalizers; it
  therefore carries the parallel pair and its induced map to the analogous data
  of \(a\)-images. It remains to see that the two whiskered comparison maps
  relating the rows,
  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\) and
  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}
  \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\), lie in \(J.W\) (equivalently, become
  isomorphisms after applying \(a\)).

  \textbf{Whiskered units are stalkwise isomorphisms.} We argue this via stalks
  of abelian-group presheaves (\(\mathrm{TopCat.Presheaf.stalk}\), which exist
  for abelian presheaves on the underlying space, unlike module-sheaf stalks).
  The sheafification unit \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} : \mathcal{P} \to a\mathcal{P}\)
  is a \emph{stalkwise isomorphism}: for each point \(x\) the stalk map
  \((\eta_{\mathcal{P}})_x : \mathcal{P}_x \to (a\mathcal{P})_x\) is an
  isomorphism (the defining property of the associated-sheaf unit). The stalk of
  a \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor presheaf is the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor of stalks,
  \((\mathcal{A} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{B})_x \cong
  \mathcal{A}_x \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{B}_x\), because the stalk is a
  filtered colimit and \(\otimes_{\mathbb{Z}}\) commutes with filtered colimits.
  Under this identification the stalk of
  \(\eta_{\mathcal{P}} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\) at \(x\) is
  \((\eta_{\mathcal{P}})_x \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\), an isomorphism
  (tensoring an isomorphism with the identity). Hence each whiskered unit is a
  stalkwise isomorphism, and a stalkwise isomorphism of abelian-group presheaves
  on a topological space is a local isomorphism, i.e.\ lies in \(J.W\)
  (\cref{lem:snap_ztensor_whisker_localIso}). Applying \(a\) therefore turns
  both whiskered comparison maps into isomorphisms.

\begin{definition}[Step-1 objectwise \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor presheaf]
  \label{def:relTensorDomainPresheaf}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.relTensorDomainPresheaf}
  \uses{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
  For presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{P}, \mathcal{Q}\) the
  assignment \(U \mapsto \mathcal{P}(U) \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{Q}(U)\),
  with restriction maps the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor of the underlying restriction
  maps, is a functor \((\mathrm{Opens}\,X)^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathrm{Ab}\). It is
  the codomain row of the relative-tensor coequalizer presentation
  (\cref{lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer}); functoriality follows from the
  functoriality of \(\mathrm{TensorProduct.map}\) (identity and composition laws
  by \(\otimes\)-induction).
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[A \(\mathbb{Z}\)-whiskered stalkwise isomorphism is a local isomorphism]
  \label{lem:snap_ztensor_whisker_localIso}
  \uses{lem:localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit, def:relTensorDomainPresheaf}
  Let \(f : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\) be a morphism of abelian-group
  presheaves on the space \(X\) that is a \emph{stalkwise isomorphism} (each
  stalk map \(f_x\) is an isomorphism), and let \(\mathcal{C}\) be a further
  abelian-group presheaf. Then the whiskered map
  \(f \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{C}}\) of \(\mathbb{Z}\)-tensor
  presheaves is again a stalkwise isomorphism, hence a local isomorphism (a
  member of \(J.W\) for the opens topology \(J\)).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:relTensorDomainPresheaf}
  The stalk functor \(\mathcal{A} \mapsto \mathcal{A}_x\) at a point \(x\) is a
  filtered colimit over the neighbourhoods of \(x\), and \(\otimes_{\mathbb{Z}}\)
  commutes with filtered colimits, so
  \((\mathcal{A} \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{C})_x \cong
  \mathcal{A}_x \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathcal{C}_x\) naturally in \(\mathcal{A}\).
  Under this identification the stalk of \(f \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\) is
  \(f_x \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\), an isomorphism since \(f_x\) is.
  Thus \(f \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{id}\) is a stalkwise isomorphism; a
  stalkwise isomorphism of abelian-group presheaves on a space is exactly a local
  isomorphism, i.e.\ lies in \(J.W\).
\end{proof}
